summary: ACCEPT: Read-only review found the model routing amendment consistent with MODEL-ROUTING-AMENDMENT-001. The default config is gpt-5.5 xhigh with multi-agent limits preserved, agent TOMLs match the new matrix, spark_worker exists with bounded mechanical scope, the orchestrator Skill includes Spark boundaries/task package/fallback/migration/reviewer gate language, docs are incremental routing updates only, FOUNDATION-001 remains accepted, and stale old-model mappings are confined to historical handoffs or closed-thread state.

tests_run.result: Confirmed branch chore/model-routing-amendment-001. Changes are concentrated in model-routing docs/config/orchestrator files plus untracked docs/26, spark-worker TOML, model-routing-state, the lead handoff, and an unrelated untracked node_modules environment artifact.
FOUNDATION-002 and FOUNDATION-008 are dependency-satisfied DRAFT candidates.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse checks for project/model-routing-state.json and orchestrator task/handoff templates

tests_run.result: Root check ran format:check, lint, typecheck, and test across apps/web, packages/core, and tools/hello; all hello-only scripts passed.

tests_run.command: rg live-rule stale model patterns across docs .agents .codex project
tests_run.result: No stale live-rule matrix entries found for old lead/systems/gameplay/QA/security high, old client medium, or old release_engineer gpt-5.4-mini patterns. Old model mentions remain only in historical handoffs or closed-thread model-routing-state context.

decisions: ACCEPT: .codex/config.toml defaults to gpt-5.5 / xhigh and preserves multi_agent=true, max_threads=6, max_depth=1, and job_max_runtime_seconds=1800.

ACCEPT: .codex/agents TOMLs match the new matrix: lead/systems/gameplay/QA/security gpt-5.5 xhigh, complex writers gpt-5.5 high, support roles gpt-5.4-mini, release_engineer and spark_worker gpt-5.3-codex-spark medium.

ACCEPT: qa_reviewer and security_reviewer remain read-only; research_scout remains read-only; spark_worker exists and is constrained to explicit mechanical path-limited tasks with required handoff and reviewer routing.
ACCEPT: The orchestrator Skill and references include Spark boundaries, required task package fields, fallback recording, migration rules, and the rule that Spark output cannot be final acceptance.
ACCEPT: Documentation changes are incremental model-routing updates and do not alter product pillars, frozen platform/architecture decisions, roadmap scope, historical policy, or M0 task facts.

ACCEPT: project/model-routing-state.json records current lead actual model/effort as unknown, records no active threads, has fallback_active=false, and its closed thread IDs match project/tasks/thread-registry.json.
ACCEPT: FOUNDATION-001 remains ACCEPTED in project/tasks/active/FOUNDATION-001.json and was not reopened solely because of routing changes.
ACCEPT: The lead handoff verification commands are substantially reproducible in read-only review; pnpm install --frozen-lockfile was intentionally not rerun because it writes dependency state.
risks: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile was not rerun during this review to preserve the user's read-only constraint; it was reported by the lead handoff and no lockfile inconsistency was found in this review.

Closed-thread actual_model/actual_effort entries in project/model-routing-state.json are not derivable from the persisted handoff JSONs alone. They are acceptable as historical operator-recorded state because thread IDs match the registry, current live actual model/effort is explicitly unknown, and these fields are not used to claim a live model switch.
Untracked node_modules/ remains present as an environment artifact and should not be staged with the routing amendment.
requested_action: Integrate this ACCEPT review for MODEL-ROUTING-AMENDMENT-001. Before commit, keep node_modules/ unstaged or ignore it, and route the next dependency-satisfied task according to the accepted model matrix.
